intro: The clearest split is that Agora uses a freemium model and Daily uses a subscription model. Agora is Real-time audio, video, and interactive live streaming SDK with global SD-RTN network infrastructure; Daily is Real-time video and audio API infrastructure for embedding calls in web and mobile applications. The data points Agora at chat applications and live collaboration features, and Daily at chat applications and live collaboration features. Pricing: Agora is freemium, free tier; Daily is subscription from $10. Lock-in is low for Agora and medium for Daily, with DX at 5/5 vs 5/5 and transparency at 4/5 vs 4/5. On listed strengths, Agora emphasises chat applications and live collaboration features, and Daily emphasises chat applications and live collaboration features. Both offer an enterprise tier for larger rollouts. Honest trade-off: neither fits static projects, so if that is your context, look elsewhere; otherwise pick on the pivotal factor above and the DX score that matters most to your team.
quick_take: Agora is for chat applications; Daily is for chat applications; decide based on which pricing model fits your budget.
verdict_a: Choose Agora if a freemium pricing model fits your procurement and your workload matches chat applications. Skip it if your context is static projects.
verdict_b: Choose Daily if a subscription pricing model fits your procurement and your workload matches chat applications. Skip it if your context is static projects.

faq.a: Entry pricing is listed as unknown for Agora and $10 for Daily. Both offer a free tier for getting started. Total cost at scale depends on usage and plan details that are not in this dataset, so confirm with each vendor.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Agora to Daily?
faq.a: Agora is tagged with low lock-in (proprietary sd-rtn network and signaling; switching requires rewrite). Daily is tagged with medium lock-in (video/audio sdk platform). Plan for SDK replacement, data export, and a parallel-run period; the dataset does not include migration tooling details.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, Agora or Daily?
faq.a: Both are rated 5/5 for developer experience in this dataset. With equal scores, the choice comes down to the specific SDK surface and docs you will use day to day. Run a short spike on each before deciding.

faq.q: Is Daily a good alternative to Agora?
faq.a: Yes for teams whose workload matches chat applications, since both list the same fit. Daily is flagged as not for static projects, so confirm your context does not fall there.
